rows.snippet: Installing Snapshot 8 and higher as a Shared Printer on Windows Server Operating Systems. 11 years ago higher-as-a-shared-printer-on-windows-server-operating-systems- Snapshot Summary When you install Laserfiche Snapshot 8 and higher on a Windows Server operating system (such as Windows S... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1011954/installing-snapshot-8-and- ...
rows.title: Installing Snapshot 8 and higher as a Shared Printer on Windows Server Operating Systems.
rows.detailTitle: Installing Snapshot 8 and higher as a Shared Printer on Windows Server Operating Systems.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Installing Snapshot 8 and higher as a Shared Printer on Windows Server Operating Systems. Product: Snapshot KB: 1011954 Published: March 31, 2008 Last Updated: July 24, 2014 Summary When you install Laserfiche Snapshot 8 and higher on a Windows Server operating system (such as Windows Server 2003), only terminal server drivers will be installed by default. Terminal server drivers only allow you to print to Snapshot from the terminal server itself and its terminal workstations. If you wish to share the printer with other workstations (such as Windows XP or Vista workstations), you will need to install Snapshot 8 with non-terminal server drivers. Resolution (Snapshot 8.2 and higher) By default, Laserfiche Snapshot 8.2 and higher is installed as a local printer. If you want Snapshot to be available as a shared printer to other computers on the network, you must choose the Custom setup type during the installation process and select the Snapshot Shared Printer subfeature. Installing Snapshot as a shared printer will prevent Snapshot from being available locally on the computer where it is installed. You must uninstall and reinstall Snapshot to change between the local version and shared printer version of Snapshot 8.2 and higher. Resolution (Snapshot 8.0 and Snapshot 8.1) To allow Snapshot 8.0 or 8.1 to be shared with Windows XP or Vista workstations, you need to manually run the installation and specify that non-terminal server drivers should be used. Please note that in this case, the shared printer will only print from the workstations, not from the Server 2003 comptuer in which it is installed. To manually install Snapshot with terminal service drivers, type the following command at a command prompt: "Path\setup.exe" /v"FORCENT=1" where Path is the path to the installation package. Note: This will install Snapshot such that it can be used as a shared printer from Windows XP or Vista workstations. Snapshot will not be able to print locally on the computer on which it is install, or on terminal workstations. If you attempt to print locally, you will receive the error, "This driver does not work with Terminal Services enabled." More Information When you install Snapshot 8 and higher on a terminal server operating system, such as Windows Server 2003 or other servers which have been configured as terminal servers), the Snapshot installation only installs terminal server drivers by default. These drivers only allow you to print from the terminal server itself or any of its terminal workstations. You cannot share the printer to other computers. Non-terminal server drivers, by contrast, allows you to share and print from remote machines that are neither the terminal server nor one of its workstations. If you have installed Snapshot onto a non-terminal server operating system (such as the default configurations of Windows XP or Vista), or you have installed Snapshot on a terminal server and will only be printing from that server or its workstations, you do not need to perform this manual installation. However, if you would like to install Snapshot on a terminal server but use it as a shared printer, this manual installation is necessary in order to install the proper drivers to use the printer as a shared printer. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

To update Laserfiche web client 11: Click the following link to download an updated version of the integration package: KB1014415_webcl... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1014415/list-of-changes-for-laserfiche-of ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Laserfiche Office Integration 11 December 2022 Update: 11.0.2212.482
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Laserfiche Office Integration 11 December 2022 Update: 11.0.2212.482
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith List of Changes for Laserfiche Office Integration 11 December 2022 Update: 11.0.2212.482 Product: Laserfiche 11 KB: 1014415 Published: December 20, 2022 Last Updated: September 25, 2023 Summary This article details the changes for Laserfiche Office Integration 11 December 2022 Update (11.0.2212.482). This update includes the following changes. You no longer receive a "Unable to load DLL 'Webview2Loader.dll" error message when attempting to attach a Laserfiche Cloud repository using Office 2019 and earlier. (400488) You now have the option to specify the installation location of a Microsoft Office application if Laserfiche is unable to automatically detect the location of the installed Office application. (304003) Resolution To update Laserfiche Windows client 11: Click the following link to download the update: KB1014415.zip Extract and run LFOfficeIntegration_x64_1102212483_KB1014415.msp or LFOfficeIntegration_x86_1102212483_KB1014415.msp depending on your configuration. To update Laserfiche web client 11: Click the following link to download an updated version of the integration package: KB1014415_webclient.zip Extract and replace the existing versions of LfWebOffice110.exe and WebOfficeBuildNum110.txt in "<web client install folder>\Web Files\OfficePlugin" with the copies included in the ZIP archive. By default, the web client's install folder is C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access. Related Links See the following Knowledge Base articles for more information on the Laserfiche 11 December 2022 update 1014407 List of Changes for Laserfiche Audit Trail 11 Update 4. 1014413 List of Changes for Laserfiche Forms 11 Update 3. Laserfiche Office Integration is also available as part of the Laserfiche 11 package. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

This software integrates with Multi-Function Devi... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013871/release-notes-for-laserfiche-for- ...
rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0
rows.detailTitle: Release Notes for Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0 Product: Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0 KB: 1013871 Published: November 6, 2017 Last Updated: April 27, 2023 Summary Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD is a single integrated solution in which scanning, searching, browsing, and printing from the Laserfiche Server can be performed. This software integrates with Multi-Function Devices (MFDs) based on the Android platform. It leverages these smart devices to provide a convenient way to communicate with your Laserfiche Server from a Ricoh MFD. Multi-Function Device Requirements The multi-function device you install Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD on must meet the following requirement: The device is based on the Android platform and supports Smart SDK 2+. The device has a Hard Disk Drive (HDD). Laserfiche Server Requirements Self-hosted Laserfiche Server 8.3 or later Laserfiche App Server 10.2 or later Installation Information You can install Laserfiche Ricoh MFD using the Ricoh Web Image Monitor. For more information about installation, see the Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0 Administration Guide included in the installation packet and on the Laserfiche Support Site. For the existing customers who want to download license of old Ricoh app, they can refer to the "Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0 Administration Guide" on how to generate license via activation tool Known Issues A printing stopped or job aborted error may occur when the document size does not match the paper size, even if auto-scaling is turned on. If this error occurs, manually select the tray that holds the appropriately sized paper under Print Source in the Printer Settings. Related Links [kb:1013872 ]1013872 List of Changes for Laserfiche for Ricoh MFD 2.0 Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: An Unhandled Exception Occurs When Specifying a Value in a Field with a Constraint when Using Web Access Scanning 8.3. 13 years ago a-value-in-a-field-with-a-constraint-when-using-web-access-scanning-8-3- Web Access 8.3 Summary When using Web Access Scanning 8.3, you may receive ... is installed.... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013019/an-unhandled-exception-occurs-whe ...
rows.title: An Unhandled Exception Occurs When Specifying a Value in a Field with a Constraint when Using Web Access Scanning 8.3.
rows.detailTitle: An Unhandled Exception Occurs When Specifying a Value in a Field with a Constraint when Using Web Access Scanning 8.3.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith An Unhandled Exception Occurs When Specifying a Value in a Field with a Constraint when Using Web Access Scanning 8.3. Product: Web Access 8.3 KB: 1013019 Published: August 9, 2012 Last Updated: August 9, 2012 Summary When using Web Access Scanning 8.3, you may receive an unhandled exception when specifying a field value in a constrained field. The error message will look similar to the following: Could not load file or assembly ''Interop.LFSO82Lib, Version=8.2.0.0, Culture-neutral, PublicKeyToken=607dd73ee2bd1c00'' or one of its dependencies. The system cannot find the file specified. Cause When validating field constraints, Web Scanning 8.3 incorrectly expects the 8.2 version of a resource that will not be available on a clean workstation where only Web Scanning 8.3 is installed. Resolution There is an updated version of the Web Scanning installation. Replace the existing Web Scanning installation package hosted on the Web Access server with the updated version. Subsequent users who download Web Scanning from Web Access will not encounter this issue. To update the hosted Web Scanning installation Click the following link to download the updated Web Scanning installation package. KB1013019.zip Extract the contents of the zip file to a temporary location. Replace the existing versions of LfWebScan83.exe and WebScanBuildNum.txt stored on the Web Access server in the "Web Files\Scanning" subfolder with the updated versions. The default path is "C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access 8\Web Files\Scanning." Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: Configuring Default Upload and Download Limits in Laserfiche Web Access and Laserfiche WebLink. 10 years ago upload-and-download-limits-in-laserfiche-web-access-and-laserfiche-weblink- Web Access 8, WebLink 8, Agenda Manager 8, Web Access 9 Summary This article explains how to increase the ... ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012682/configuring-default-upload-and-do ...
rows.title: Configuring Default Upload and Download Limits in Laserfiche Web Access and Laserfiche WebLink.
rows.detailTitle: Configuring Default Upload and Download Limits in Laserfiche Web Access and Laserfiche WebLink.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Configuring Default Upload and Download Limits in Laserfiche Web Access and Laserfiche WebLink. Product: Web Access 8, WebLink 8, Agenda Manager 8, Web Access 9 KB: 1012682 Published: October 5, 2010 Last Updated: August 13, 2015 Summary This article explains how to increase the default upload and download limits for Laserfiche Web Access and WebLink. It is only necessary to complete the steps listed below if the default limits are not sufficient. Note: Though Laserfiche Agenda Manager is web-based, it will not be covered in this article, as it has an upload/download limit of 100MB, which is sufficient for virtually all situations. Note: Some browsers define their own limits. For example, Internet Explorer 6 limits downloads to 2GBs, while Internet Explorer 7 has a limit of 4GBs (for more information on Internet Explorer limits, see Microsoft Knowledge Base Article 298618). Important: The steps below require you to edit Web Access'' and/or WebLink's web.config file, which, if edited incorrectly, may cause the software to stop running. As a precaution, back up the original copy of this file (each file's location will be listed in the sections below) before making any changes. If your change causes problems, replace the edited file with the original. Web Access Limits By default, Web Access allows users to: Upload files that are no larger than 2,097,151KBs (2GB; unless you are on IIS 7, in which case the default is 30 MB) and take no longer than 3,600 seconds (1 hour) to upload. Export files that are no larger than 204,800KBs (200MB) and take no longer than 7,200 seconds (2 hours) to download. E-mail files that are no larger than 204,800KBs (200MB) and take no longer than 110 seconds (less than 2 minutes) to download. Instructions on modifying these settings will NOT be covered in this article, as increasing the default e-mail limits is not recommended. To modify the settings above, open the Web.config file, which is in Web Access'' installation directory (in most cases, this will be <Installation Drive>\Program Files\Laserfiche\Web Access 8\Web Files), and follow the necessary instructions below. Note: In order for a change to take effect, restart Internet Information Services (IIS) or restart the application pool hosting Web Access. Note: In the code blocks listed below, some of the initial values may vary if the Web.config file has previously been edited. To modify upload limits: In Web.config, locate the following code block: <location path="Dialogs/UploadFilePage.aspx"> <system.web> <neatUpload useHttpModule="true" /> <!-- By Default This allows uploads of sizes up to 2GB --> <httpRuntime maxRequestLength="2097151" executionTimeout="3600" /> </system.web> </location> If necessary, change the value of the maxRequestLength setting to the maximum number of KBs a file can be before it cannot be uploaded via Web Access. We do not recommend setting this value greater than 2,097,151KBs (2GB). In the previous step, if you changed the maxRequestLength setting to a value larger than 30,720KBs (30MBs), and if you are using IIS 7 or higher, add the following code block directly under the <system.webServer> section. Modify the maxAllowedContentLength setting to ensure it matches the maxRequestLength setting. We do not recommend setting this value greater than 2,097,151KBs (2GB). <security> <requestFiltering> <requestLimits maxAllowedContentLength="X" /> </requestFiltering> </security> If necessary, change the value of the executionTimeout setting to the maximum number of seconds that can pass during the upload process before the file cannot be uploaded via Web Access. For Web Access 9, you should also append the following block below the UploadFilePage.aspx code block. <location path="Dialogs/DropInFile.aspx"> <system.web> <!-- By Default This allows uploads of sizes up to 2GB --> <httpRuntime maxRequestLength="2097151" executionTimeout="3600" /> </system.web> </location> If you want to also modify the upload timeout for Web Access Light, append the following code block below the UploadFilePage.aspx code block. <location path="Light/ImportAction.aspx"> <system.web> <!-- By Default This allows uploads to go on for 1 hour --> <httpRuntime executionTimeout="3600" /> </system.web> </location> If necessary, change the value of the executionTimeout setting to the maximum number of seconds that can pass during the upload process before the file cannot be uploaded via Web Access Light. Save your changes and close the text editor. To modify export limits: In Web.config, locate the following code block: <location path="Dialogs/Export/GetExportFile.aspx"> <system.web> <!-- By Default This allows downloads to go on for 2 hours --> <httpRuntime executionTimeout="7200" /> </system.web> </location> If necessary, change the value of the executionTimeout setting to the maximum number of seconds that can pass during the export process before the file cannot be exported via Web Access. If you need to c

WebLink Administrator&#... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012585/release-notes-for-weblink- ...
rows.title: Release Notes for WebLink 8.0.2.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in Release Notes for WebLink 8.0.2. Product: WebLink 8.0.2 KB: 1012585 Published: May 3, 2010 Last Updated: May 3, 2010 Summary Web Link 8.0.2 is a maintenance release that adds support for the new Web Distribution Portal licensing model. It also includes bug fixes implemented since the release of Web Link 8.0.1. This article covers: Introductory Installation Information System Specifications Compatibility Information Known Issues Installation Information 1012644 The WebLink 8 Installation May Not Successfully Create the "WebLink 8 Reporting" Virtual Directory. (47936) The WebLink 8.0.2 installation can be run on top of your existing WebLink 8.0 or WebLink 8.0.1 installations. The installation will not let you proceed if it does not detect IIS or ASP.NET. Make sure those Windows features are enabled prior to installing WebLink. The installation will install .NET Framework 3.5 if it is not already installed on the Web server. However, if you are using Windows Server 2008 R2, you must manually enable the .NET Framework 3.5.1 feature prior to installing WebLink. When upgrading from a previous version of WebLink 8 to WebLink 8.0.2, you will be prompted for your WebLink 8 license file during the installation. (57818) When upgrading from a previous version of WebLink 8 to WebLink 8.0.2, you will be prompted to reenter IIS credentials during the installation. (57878) Installing Language Packs The WebLink 8.0.2 Language Packs are bundled together in one installation package separate from the main WebLink installation. The DVD autorun contains an option to launch the WebLink 8.0.2 Language Packs installation. During the installation, you can specify which language packs you wish to install. The bundled installation includes the following language packs: Arabic Chinese (Simplified) Chinese (Traditional) French Italian Portuguese (Brazil) Spanish Vietnamese System Specifications Minimum Specifications CPU: 2 GHz or faster processor Memory: 1 GB RAM Operating system: Windows XP (Service Pack 2) with IIS 5, Windows Server 2003 (Service Pack 1) with IIS 6, Windows Vista with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 with IIS 7, Windows 7 with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 R2 with IIS 7.5 Recommended Specifications CPU: 2.8 GHz or faster processor Memory: 2 GB RAM or more Operating System: Windows Server 2003 with IIS 6, Windows Server 2008 with IIS 7, Windows Server 2008 R2 with IIS 7.5 Compatibility Information Laserfiche Server 8: Laserfiche WebLink 8 requires version 8.0 or later of the Laserfiche Server. Web Link is not compatible with standalone editions of Laserfiche (Executive, Desktop, etc.). Web Server: WebLink is an ASP.NET 2.0 Web application for IIS. If you are running IIS 7, make sure the IIS Metabase and IIS 6 configuration compatibility (under IIS 6 Management Compatibility) feature is enabled for your IIS 7 installation. Client Workstation: Web Link is best viewed using Internet Explorer 6, Internet Explorer 7, Internet Explorer 8, Firefox 2, Firefox 3, Safari, Chrome, and Opera. Known Issues Component Known Issue WebLink Administrator's Utility An "Error: Could not load file or assembly ''System.Core, Version=3.5.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b77a5c561934e089'' or one of its dependencies. The system cannot find the file specified" error message displays when trying to load the WebLink Administrator's utility on a computer with Windows Server 2008 R2. Windows Server 2008 R2 does not allow 3rd-party installations to install .NET Framework 3.5. If you are using Windows Server 2008 R2, you must manually enable the .NET Framework 3.5.1 Features option from the Add Features Wizard in the Server Manager. (57885) WebLink Administrator's Utility Virtual Directories cannot be created in the WebLink Administration Utility (43340). WebLink Administrator's Utility Configuring one repository to automatically log in with Windows Authentication using the accessing user's account can conflict with another repository configured to automatically log in with Windows Authentication using an anonymous user account. These 2 different configurations require IIS to be set up in different ways (48304). WebLink Designer When WebLink is uninstalled and reinstalled, the settings for the Search Results customized in the WebLink Designer are not retained. Even though the settings will appear to be your specific customizations before uninstalling, you need to resave the settings in order to retain them (47726). WebLink Designer The Theme settings are not retained when you uninstall and reinstall WebLink (47728). WebLink Designer The WebLink Designer does not remember the last state it was in after being closed and reopened (44847). WebLink Users may run into a "lost connection has been detected" or "too many connections" error when accessing a WebLink site installed on a Windows XP machine. Increasing the limit IIS allows will fix this issue. This happens more often when using Internet Explorer 8 (46685). WebLink The Im

rows.snippet: Searches Cannot be Performed on Index Card Field Values With Multiple Sequential Spaces. 22 years ago performed-on-index-card-field-values-with-multiple-sequential-spaces- WebLink 6 Summary Searches cannot be performed for list field data containing multiple sequential spaces. For exampl... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1000563/searches-cannot-be-perform ...
rows.title: Searches Cannot be Performed on Index Card Field Values With Multiple Sequential Spaces.
rows.detailTitle: Searches Cannot be Performed on Index Card Field Values With Multiple Sequential Spaces.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Searches Cannot be Performed on Index Card Field Values With Multiple Sequential Spaces. Product: WebLink 6 KB: 1000563 Published: May 17, 2004 Last Updated: May 17, 2004 Summary Searches cannot be performed for list field data containing multiple sequential spaces. For example, the term "RE: Vice" will not return search results. Cause LaserFiche Client does not trim list field items of extra spaces. However, when searching through WebLink, previous versions would trim off blank spaces. Resolution Upgrade to WebLink 6.15 to remove this limitation. More Information For example, suppose that a list field in the Client contains the entry, "RE: Vice." Typing the preceding text in the WebLink template search will not return any results because WebLink is searching for "RE: Vice." Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.snippet: List of Changes for Web Access 9.0.2 Service Pack 1. 12 years ago Service Pack 1. July 8, 2013 | KB: 1013277 https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013277/list-of-changes-for-web-access-9-0-2-service-pack-1- Web Access 9.0.2 ... previously imported into an encryp... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1013277/list-of-changes-for-web-ac ...
rows.title: List of Changes for Web Access 9.0.2 Service Pack 1.
rows.detailTitle: List of Changes for Web Access 9.0.2 Service Pack 1.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Sign in List of Changes for Web Access 9.0.2 Service Pack 1. Product: Web Access 9.0.2 KB: 1013277 Published: July 8, 2013 Last Updated: July 8, 2013 Summary This article details service pack 1 (hotfix 1013277) for Laserfiche Web Access 9.0.2. The service pack includes the following changes: You no longer receive a "NaN is not a valid value for Int32" error message when you drag and drop thumbnail images past the last page in the document. (101562) The Versions tab in the Metadata Viewer and Document Viewer now displays properly with various Web Access language packs. (102250) Quick search selections that include a custom quick search are now saved after you log out and log back in. (102359) Web Access Scanning no longer becomes unstable in certain situations when you scan into a document that contains both a template with a dynamic field and a field that is independent of the template. (102557) When using Chrome, list fields with no assigned value no longer display as having the first value selected. (102562) The Refresh toolbar button in the Document Viewer now refreshes the Image Pane after you use the Generate Pages option to regenerate a new set of image files for PDF documents. (102601) The E-mail Documents option is now available for electronic document shortcuts. (103172) Web Access now properly displays image pages for documents that were previously imported into an encrypted volume as part of a Laserfiche briefcase. (103306) The Laserfiche Search Connector for Windows 7 now properly returns search results. (103590) The Links tab in the Metadata Viewer and Document Viewer can now display entry links to a folder. (103922) The Web Access update includes the following files: WebAccess.dll (9.0.2.744) WebAccessCommon.dll (9.0.2.744) WebAccessResources.dll (9.0.2.744) ImportDocument.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) Metadata.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) NewDocument.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) NewFolder.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) VersionFields.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) wa90.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) webpart.js (Last modified on July 1, 2013) More Information Note: This update is included as part of the Laserfiche Rio 9.0.2 installation packages available on the Laserfiche Support Site after July 5th, 2013. The updated Web Access and Web Import Services installation packages contain an "Updates" subfolder with .msp patch files. When using the updated installation to install Web Access 9.0.2 for the first time, the installation process will automatically detect and install the appropriate .msp files located in those subfolders. If you have not yet installed Laserfiche Rio 9.0.2, please download the most recent version of the Laserfiche Rio package. Click the following link to download a zip file containing the hotfix. KB1013277.zip The zip file contains WebAccess902744_SP1_20130705.exe. Double-click the .exe to update Web Access. Note: The zip file also contains Microsoft Windows Installer patch files (.msp) if you want to update Web Access, the DocuSign web service, and the Laser App web service individually. Related Links Service Pack 1 (hotfix 1013277) is a cumulative update that supersedes Knowledge Base article 1013240. KB1013277.zip includes the Web Import Services files and Web Scanning installation package included in article 1013240. 1013240 List of Changes for Web Access 9.0.2 Hotfix 1013240. 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

rows.title: The LaserFiche Snapshot Service Requires the .NET Framework.
rows.detailTitle: The LaserFiche Snapshot Service Requires the .NET Framework.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith The LaserFiche Snapshot Service Requires the .NET Framework. Product: Snapshot 6 KB: 1000552 Published: May 6, 2004 Last Updated: May 6, 2004 Summary The LaserFiche Snapshot Service service is a .NET program. As such, if the .NET framework is not installed, the service cannot run. More Information While the Snapshot service is a .NET program, the rest of Snapshot is not. If .NET is not installed, you may receive errors involving error code 1053. The Snapshot service is only needed when the Snapshot printer is shared over the network. It is used to process print jobs coming over the network. Printing to Snapshot locally is possible even if the service is not running. Share 2026 Laserfiche Privacy Trademark

(41363) Snapshot If Snapshot 7 and Snapshot 8 are installed on the same machine, the Snapshot Configuration (Current User) ... https://support.laserfiche.com/kb/1012558/release-notes-for-laserfiche-8-1- ...
rows.title: Release Notes for Laserfiche 8.1.2.
rows.detailText: Product Support Learn Blake Smith Release Notes for Laserfiche 8.1.2. Product: Laserfiche 8.1.2 KB: 1012558 Published: April 5, 2010 Last Updated: April 5, 2010 Summary This article includes release notes for Laserfiche 8.1.2. More Information See the following list of known issues for version 8.1.2. Product Issue Administration Console Unregistering and reregistering a repository resets the location of the audit logs. (34062) Audit Trail Audit Trail 8.0 cannot import Laserfiche 8.1 audit logs that contain any events new to Laserfiche 8.1. Audit Trail The "Configure Laserfiche Audit Trail 8" and "Laserfiche Audit Trail 8 Main Page" links in the Start menu do not have the correct icons. (36038) Audit Trail Large legends can potentially obscure chart data. (34888) Client 1012571 The Laserfiche Client 8.1.2 Installation Package May Not Properly Upgrade LFImage81.ocx If The Version From Hotfix 1012536 Is Installed. Client You may be unable to extract or OCR a document during the document creation/import process if you are creating/importing the document into a frozen record folder. As a workaround, you can import (along with OCR) into a different folder, then move the record into the frozen record folder. (47575) Client The Laserfiche Client may become unstable when more than one instance of the Client is open on the same machine and both instances are OCRing large documents. (46582) Client 1011989 You May Be Unable to Modify Your Laserfiche Installation From Programs and Features on Windows Vista or Windows Server 2008. (37344) Client Certain large images can cause the Laserfiche Client to display error 6602. Generating thumbnails for these images use up too much system resources. (41991) Client You may receive a "Cannot find file [6721] error" when you try to open an empty (0 byte) electronic document. (41289) Client Certain field constraint masks may not display in the Laserfiche Client. (39598) Client The Laserfiche Client does not allow you to specify a default field token if the name of the token exceeds the defined length of the field. (39382) Client You may need to restart you computer after installing the Laserfiche Client. (36106) Client Opacity levels for image annotations may not display correctly in exported PDF's. (35697) Client The "Recent Documents" listing in the File menu can truncate path information using a ellipsis. This means that documents with the same name that are located in different parts of your repository may appear as duplicate items in the "Recent Documents" listing. (35149) Client Laserfiche may be unable to export large PDF's when the Convert annotations into PDF annotations option is enabled. (35106) Client There may be certain annotation display issues when using the Laserfiche Client through Remote Desktop. For example, a sticky note may display as black even though you chose a different color. However, after saving changes, the sticky note appears as the desired color. Another example is that attachment annotation icons may not display properly.(33325, 34844) Client You cannot remove the assigned template when you multi-select multiple documents. (26049) Client Pressing the SPACEBAR will not automatically populate a date/time field if the field already contains a value. (28005) PhotoDocs The Previous and Next toolbar buttons on the Manual Correction window may not smoothly skip deleted images if you delete images from the source directory while the window is currently open. (47416) PhotoDocs The PhotoDocs image enhancement options may be unable to process certain images with white text on a black background. (37937) Scanning 1012020 An "Element ''PageRange'' was not found." Error Occurs When Launching Laserfiche Scanning in Basic Mode. (39960) Scanning Laserfiche Scanning does not support setting the ENTER key as a keyboard shortcut. (43485) Scanning A user with the Manage Metadata privilege may be unable to send a document to Laserfiche if the assigned template has a field with a default value and the user does not have the Create field access right on that field. (40185) Scanning When reloading Laserfiche Scanning after a crash, you can choose to recover documents that have not been sent to Laserfiche. Template data may not properly display for the recovered documents. However, this is only a display issue and the data is not lost. When you send the documents to your repository, the template data will be properly saved. (37730) Scanning Laserfiche Scanning can recover scanned documents after a crash. However, Scanning may not recover unique document names. (36258) Scanning Laserfiche Scanning and the OCR engine are both components of the Laserfiche Client installation. During the Laserfiche Client installation, if you choose to install Laserfiche Scanning, but do not choose to install the OCR engine, the OCR process in Laserfiche Scanning will be available. However, if you configure your scanning process with an OCR process, it will silently fail. (35524) Scanning The Pap

(34062) Audit Trail Audit Trail 8.0 cannot import Laserfiche 8.1 audit logs that contain any events new to Laserfiche 8.1. Another example is that attachment annotation icons may not display properly.(33325, 34844) Client You cannot remove the assigned template when you multi-select multiple documents. (47416) PhotoDocs The PhotoDocs image enhancement options may be unable to process certain images with white text on a black background. (37937) Scanning 1012020 An "Element ''PageRange'' was not found." Error Occurs When Launching Laserfiche Scanning in Basic Mode. (39960) Scanning Laserfiche Scanning does not support setting the ENTER key as a keyboard shortcut. (43485) Scanning You may receive a "The command line ''C:\Program Files\Laserfiche\Client 8\Scanning\LFScan.exe -c -shutdown'' was invalid. Use default options?" error message if you close Laserfiche Scanning while there are still documents, choose to delete the documents when prompted, and then immediately close the Laserfiche Client window. After you click OK, you may receive another error message about the Laserfiche Client not running. You can safely ignore these two error messages in this situation. (40185) Scanning When reloading Laserfiche Scanning after a crash, you can choose to recover documents that have not been sent to Laserfiche. Template data may not properly display for the recovered documents. However, this is only a display issue and the data is not lost.
